prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms) using GFN2-xTB in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Three states computed: neutral (N), cation (N+1, doublet), and anion (N−1, doublet) to compute Fukui descriptors. The most electrophilic site is the iron center with f⁺ = 0.22885456 (the most nucleophilic site is also the iron center with f⁻ = 0.25938191). The dual descriptor at iron is −0.030527354, indicating nucleophilic preference. All carbons (ring atoms) and hydrogens show much lower reactivity. SCF converged for all three electronic states; charge conservation checks passed. Method: GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding via xtb program. Geometry: input structure used as-is (not DFT-relaxed). Output files: result JSON at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp7ctvc0gw/live.json` and PNG bar chart showing f⁺/f⁻/Δf per atom. No warnings reported; integrity trustworthy.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
